what_it_does: A self-contained interactive HTML scenario player that renders a warm-gold themed conversational AI interface inside a toggleable phone or desktop frame, and lets a viewer step through live states (idle, auto-listen, push-to-talk, thinking, reply, consent, running, minimized, empty), switch personas (Vi, Atlas, Nova), and observe motion and aurora intensity changes driven by CSS custom properties and a small embedded JS state machine.
observations: Warm charcoal base with gold as the primary identity color; no green anywhere.
Personas are pastel sub-families within the gold world: Vi (canonical gold), Atlas (apricot), Nova (rose-gold).
State is orthogonal and carried on #root via data-scenario, data-persona, and data-frame attributes.
CSS custom properties are used as tokens; persona re-skins override --acc, --acc-deep, --acc-glow, --acc-flow, and --orb-hi.
Ambient motion uses always-on infinite keyframes; entrance stagger replays on scenario switch via a .replay/.go class dance.
Scenario visibility is gated with .only classes keyed off #root[data-scenario].
Aurora intensity is driven by the --activity CSS variable (0 calm to 1 alive).
Includes distinct voice-mode affordances: auto-listen (glowing mic, concentric ripples) versus push-to-talk (held button with level-meter bars).
Contains a consent/propose card with approve/decline actions, a running progress strip, an indicate chip, and a minimized floating presence pill.
Desktop frame adds left/right gutters representing the composition canvas and inbox/run surfaces.
Uses Google Fonts (Fraunces, IBM Plex Mono, IBM Plex Sans) and an inline SVG data-URI noise grain layer.
Comments explicitly map UI elements to real capabilities (RhmChat.tsx / Settings.tsx).
Includes data-ui-ref attributes on many elements, suggesting a UI-reference registry.
Keyboard arrow keys cycle through scenarios.
standards: State machine expressed almost entirely through data-* attributes and CSS selectors rather than class manipulation.
Persona theming implemented by scoped CSS custom-property overrides on #root.
Activity-driven ambient background (aurora) tied to a single --activity variable.
Push-to-talk rendered as a distinct held-gesture control with animated level bars, separate from auto-listen.
conventions: Follows a token-based design system using CSS custom properties.
Uses semantic HTML and BEM-ish class naming.
Keeps the prototype self-contained in a single HTML file.
Documents design rationale and real-capability mappings in extensive HTML comments.

Breaks: some keyframes are placeholders (orbflow does nothing), inline style overrides exist for the PTT input bar, emoji are used as functional icons, and there is no visible prefers-reduced-motion handling.
concerns: The orbflow keyframe is a no-op placeholder, suggesting unfinished motion detail.
Scenario names and activity values are duplicated between CSS selectors and the JS SCENES map.
No ARIA labels or roles on many interactive controls (stepper buttons, persona faces, frame toggle, icon buttons).
Emoji glyphs used as status icons may render inconsistently across platforms.
Desktop frame uses transform:scale() to fit small viewports, which can make interaction targets tiny.
The minimized pill event listener is attached unconditionally even when the element is hidden.
Heavy reliance on #root[data-scenario] selectors may make future state combinations brittle.
notes: A drivable, single-file motion prototype of the RHM chat surface that encodes how color, persona, voice mode, and activity state should look and feel on both phone and desktop frames.
questions: How do the persona voice engines referenced in Settings:213-231 map to the Vi/Atlas/Nova color families shown here?
Are the data-ui-ref values consumed by an external test harness, design linter, or corpus indexer?
What is the intended handoff path from this exploration mockup to the production RhmChat.tsx and Settings.tsx components?
Is the warm-gold palette already defined in tokens.json, or is this file proposing new token values?
How should reduced-motion preferences be handled in the animated production implementation?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is an interactive scenario player for judging the living instrument by sight and motion; the file is exactly that — a standalone, drivable HTML prototype with controls, animated states, and device framing.
apparent_themes: Presence surfacing through light and orb
intention_signals: It is trying to give reviewers a feelable, drivable preview of the RHM UI across personas and device frames so that design decisions about color, motion, voice-mode affordances, and the consent gate can be judged experientially rather than from static frames.
novelty: Combines a scenario stepper, persona color-token swapper, phone/desktop frame toggle, and activity-driven aurora into one self-contained motion prototype that explicitly maps each state to real product capabilities.
contribution: A concrete, interactive motion reference for the RHM chat surface that encodes state-to-animation mappings, persona color tokens, voice-mode distinctions, and the consent-gate UX pattern in a form that can be stepped through without a backend.
summary_for_embedding: Self-contained interactive HTML mockup of a warm-gold AI companion chat interface with a scenario stepper, persona recoloring, phone/desktop frame toggle, and activity-driven ambient motion.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a drivable motion prototype for reviewing RHM chat states, persona palettes, voice modes, and consent interactions.
